content:
type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need Encoding.encode() in core.py, so full file disjointness is impossible. Proposal to avoid clobbering: both params are independent optional kwargs with safe defaults. I will add return_positions as the LAST keyword-only parameter in the encode() signature (after your transformers param, whatever you name it), and gate my position-tracking logic in its own 'if return_positions:' block placed at the END of the method body, after the existing try/except return logic (I'll restructure minimally to capture the result first, then branch). Please add your transformers param/logic earlier in the signature and body. This keeps our diffs on mostly distinct lines. I will not touch encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch, or any other method. Let me know if this works or propose adjustments.
